reason: Both answers correctly diagnose the Goodhart's law dynamic, note activated users are roughly flat, and recommend paying on activation instead of signup, plus segmentation and fraud checks. B edges ahead by flagging the alternative hypothesis (check if organic activation also dropped, meaning the funnel broke rather than the referral program) and by naming a clear decision metric (cost per activated user) to benchmark against other channels. A is slightly more concise but adds nothing B lacks. The gap is modest but B is marginally more useful.
